question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the /mempool endpoint with high-frequency HTTP requests, exploiting the fact that mempoolAPIService has no rate limiting or request throttling, causing CPU/goroutine exhaustion that crashes 30% or more of rosetta processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the /mempool/transaction endpoint with concurrent requests, exploiting the absence of any concurrency control in mempoolAPIService, to exhaust goroutine pools across multiple rosetta nodes and cause >= 30% of them to become unresponsive?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with oversized JSON bodies to the /mempool endpoint, exploiting the fact that the Rosetta SDK parses types.NetworkRequest before the handler discards it, causing unbounded memory allocation across multiple nodes and triggering OOM-based node shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with oversized JSON bodies to the /mempool/transaction endpoint, exploiting the SDK's parsing of types.MempoolTransactionRequest before the handler discards it, causing memory pressure that degrades or crashes >= 30% of rosetta nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that Mempool() ignores its context.Context parameter (using _ context.Context), meaning request cancellation is never propagated, to hold server goroutines open indefinitely during slow-read attacks and exhaust the goroutine pool on >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that MempoolTransaction() ignores its context.Context parameter (using _ context.Context), meaning client disconnection is never detected, to keep server-side goroutines alive after client disconnect and exhaust resources on multiple nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NewMempoolAPIService()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated instantiation paths through the /mempool route, exploiting the stateless mempoolAPIService struct{} which has no singleton enforcement, to amplify per-request overhead across all registered rosetta nodes and degrade >= 30% of them?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user use HTTP/2 multiplexing to send thousands of concurrent streams to the /mempool endpoint on a single TCP connection, exploiting the lack of per-stream rate limiting in mempoolAPIService, to exhaust server-side stream handling capacity and crash >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user use HTTP/2 multiplexing to send thousands of concurrent streams to the /mempool/transaction endpoint, exploiting the absence of stream-level throttling in mempoolAPIService, to exhaust server resources and cause >= 30% of rosetta nodes to stop processing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user send malformed JSON payloads to the /mempool endpoint, exploiting the SDK's JSON decoder which must attempt to parse types.NetworkRequest before the handler returns ErrNotImplemented, to cause repeated decoder panics or excessive CPU usage that degrades >= 30% of nodes?
